title: Reading: Shopping and Numbers
description: Read a short story about shopping in Delhi to practice numbers and colors.

blocks.passage:
📖 खरीदारी (Kharidaari - Shopping)

मैं दिल्ली में हूँ। (Main Delhi mein hoon.) - I am in Delhi.
यह कलम है। (Yah kalam hai.) - This is a pen.
लाल कलम है। (Laal kalam hai.) - It's a red pen.

कलम दस रुपये है। (Kalam das rupaye hai.) - The pen is 10 rupees.
किताब तीस रुपये है। (Kitaab tees rupaye hai.) - The book is 30 rupees.

वह क्या है? (Vah kya hai?) - What is that?
वह गाड़ी है। (Vah gaadi hai.) - That is a car.
नीली गाड़ी है। (Neeli gaadi hai.) - It's a blue car.

मैं एक कलम और एक किताब खरीदता हूँ। (Main ek kalam aur ek kitaab khareedta hoon.) - I buy a pen and a book.
चालीस रुपये। (Chaalis rupaye.) - 40 rupees.
धन्यवाद। (Dhanyavaad.) - Thank you.

questions.q: How much does the book cost?

items.explanation: No, कलम दस रुपये है (The pen is 10 rupees).

items.statement: The person buys the blue car.
items.explanation: No, they only buy the pen and book.

blocks.title: Currency in India
blocks.content: The currency used in India is the Indian Rupee (INR - ₹). In Hindi, it's called 'Rupaya' (singular) or 'Rupaye' (plural). While bargaining is common in street markets, fixed prices are expected in malls and modern shops.
